final_answer: Immediately throttle the password‑reset endpoint: apply per‑IP and per‑account rate limits and return HTTP 429 when limits are exceeded. Add a CAPTCHA challenge to the reset form to stop automated abuse. Instruct clients to use exponential back‑off with jitter on retries to avoid a thundering herd. Decouple token generation and email delivery by queuing them to a background worker, and cache recent reset tokens in Redis to prevent duplicate DB writes. Optimize the database by adding an index on the email/username lookup column and scaling read capacity with additional read replicas and larger connection pools. Deploy edge‑level rate limiting (e.g., via CDN or firewall) to cap POSTs per IP, and configure the service to emit detailed alerts when request volume spikes, enabling rapid detection and response.
